nodes.title: Relationships Preserve Knowledge
nodes.summary: Knowledge survives movement between tools when relationships carry meaning, evidence, authorship, and addressable context instead of collapsing into anonymous links.

nodes.title: XanaNode Contributors
nodes.summary: The people and future maintainers who author, review, preserve, and project XanaNode-compatible substrates.

nodes.title: Knowledge Substrate
nodes.summary: The graph-shaped structure that lets information remain understandable across time by making claims, sources, media, fragments, and relationships explicit.

nodes.title: Protocol Artifacts
nodes.summary: The portable JSON files, schemas, fragments, reports, and media references that allow tools to validate, compare, mount, import, merge, and render substrates.

nodes.title: Substrate Projection Layer
nodes.summary: A renderer or lens that presents a substrate for a particular use without becoming the substrate itself.

nodes.title: XanaNode
nodes.summary: A protocol for independently authored knowledge substrates that preserve relationships, provenance, lineage, disagreement, and addressable fragments.

nodes.title: What Is XanaNode?
nodes.summary: An explanation of XanaNode as a layered protocol, not a single viewer, website, repository, or graph visualization.

nodes.title: XanaNode Canonical Pack Bootstrap
nodes.summary: The point where Core begins shipping a mountable canonical XanaNode substrate pack as protocol JSON.

nodes.title: Relationships Preserve Knowledge Sentence
nodes.summary: Relationships preserve knowledge.

nodes.title: XanaNode Icon
nodes.summary: The XanaNode project icon used as primary media for XanaNode nodes and projection branding.
nodes.alt: A connected node mark with orange and blue paths crossing around a bright central node.
nodes.creator: Built By Bots

nodes.title: Markdown Recreation Friction
nodes.summary: Forcing authors to recreate validated protocol nodes as Markdown creates duplicate governance surfaces and loses the benefit of portable substrate artifacts.

nodes.title: Built By Bots
nodes.summary: The authoring and project identity associated with XanaNode development and media attribution.

nodes.title: Cytoscape Consortium
nodes.summary: The organization and community associated with Cytoscape and Cytoscape.js development.

nodes.title: Hugo Authors
nodes.summary: The open source project community responsible for Hugo.

nodes.title: Christian Siefen
nodes.summary: The personally identified author and maintainer of the canonical XanaNode substrate.

nodes.title: World Wide Web
nodes.summary: The public networked space where XanaNode domains, repositories, documentation, and published substrates are reached.

nodes.title: XanaNode Core SDK
nodes.summary: The renderer-independent SDK that validates substrates, resolves mounted packs, analyzes intake, builds fragments, generates suggestions, and exports protocol artifacts.

nodes.title: XanaNode Hugo Theme
nodes.summary: A static website projection layer that uses Hugo to read XanaNode protocol artifacts and present pages, graph navigation, trails, sources, media, fragments, and review utilities.

nodes.title: XanaNode Protocol
nodes.summary: The specification layer that defines substrate artifacts, node types, relationship types, addressing, pack ingress, federation, governance, and validation rules.

nodes.title: XanaNode Workspace
nodes.summary: A coordination layer for local authoring, mounted pack selection, media placement, import review, Git workflows, and stack-level health checks.

nodes.title: XNP-0004 Merge Validation
nodes.summary: A protocol proposal describing merge validation for independently authored substrates.

nodes.title: Protocol Implements Relationship Preservation
nodes.summary: A first-class relationship node representing that the XanaNode protocol implements the claim that relationships preserve knowledge.

nodes.title: Canonical Pack v0.1.0
nodes.summary: The first Core-bundled revision of the canonical XanaNode pack.

nodes.title: Author Profile Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for identifying substrate authors, maintainers, and provenance-bearing author profiles.

nodes.title: Author Profile Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for author-profile.

nodes.title: Compatibility Report Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for compatibility-report.

nodes.title: Merge Report Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for merge-report.

nodes.title: Nanopublication Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for nanopublication.

nodes.title: Ro Crate Metadata Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for ro-crate-metadata.

nodes.title: Substrate Diff Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for substrate-diff.

nodes.title: Substrate Manifest Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for substrate-manifest.

nodes.title: Substrate Node Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for substrate-node.

nodes.title: Substrate Relationships Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for substrate-relationships.

nodes.title: Xananode Node Types Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for xananode-node-types.

nodes.title: Xananode Property Registry Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for xananode-property-registry.

nodes.title: Xananode Relationship Types Schema
nodes.summary: Canonical schema registry entry for xananode-relationship-types.

nodes.title: Canonical Schemas Registry
nodes.summary: The protocol registry that identifies canonical schema artifacts and their current versions.

nodes.title: Compatibility Report Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for describing whether schemas, packs, substrates, or tools can interoperate without destructive conversion.

nodes.title: Known Implementations Registry
nodes.summary: The protocol registry of tools, renderers, validators, and substrates that declare XanaNode compatibility.

nodes.title: Merge Report Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for reporting merge decisions, identity handling, conflicts, and intake outcomes between substrates.

nodes.title: Federation Merge Report Example Namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace used for merge report and mapping records in the federation example.

nodes.title: Minimal Example Namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace used by the minimal example substrate.

nodes.title: Museum Extension Example Namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace used by the custom extension example substrate.

nodes.title: Federation Example A Namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace used by the first independently authored federation example substrate.

nodes.title: Federation Example B Namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace used by the second independently authored federation example substrate.

nodes.title: Namespace Registry
nodes.summary: The protocol registry of known namespace identifiers, owners, and extension vocabularies.

nodes.title: Namespace Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for declaring interoperable XanaNode namespaces.

nodes.title: XanaNode Core
nodes.summary: Canonical namespace for core XanaNode node and relationship types.

nodes.title: Nanopublication Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for representing assertion, provenance, and publication information in nanopublication-compatible form.

nodes.title: Claim
nodes.summary: A specific assertion that can be supported, disputed, revised, deprecated, or rejected.

nodes.title: Community
nodes.summary: A group organized around an identity, platform, shared work, practice, interest, or tradition.

nodes.title: Concept
nodes.summary: An idea, pattern, theory, failure mode, or explanatory structure. A concept is built from claims.

nodes.title: Essay
nodes.summary: A human-authored argument that connects claims, sources, observations, and concepts.

nodes.title: Event
nodes.summary: Something that happened at a time and optionally at a place.

nodes.title: Fragment
nodes.summary: A stable, addressable portion of another node, source, media object, or publication used for deep linking, quotation, citation, transclusion, and revision-aware reference.

nodes.title: Media
nodes.summary: An image, video, audio clip, screenshot, diagram, document, or other media item with provenance.

nodes.title: Observation
nodes.summary: A real-world case, symptom, example, incident, failure, or pattern being analyzed.

nodes.title: Organization
nodes.summary: A company, institution, agency, lab, school, collective, standards body, nonprofit, or group.

nodes.title: Person
nodes.summary: A human actor who created, influenced, witnessed, argued, documented, or participated in something.

nodes.title: Place
nodes.summary: A physical or virtual location where people, projects, organizations, or events exist or occurred.

nodes.title: Project
nodes.summary: A built, proposed, fictional, abandoned, or active system with a purpose and lineage.

nodes.title: Publication
nodes.summary: A released intellectual or creative work such as a book, article, paper, film, episode, or talk.

nodes.title: Node Type Registry
nodes.summary: The canonical registry of current XanaNode node types, subtypes, visual treatment hints, and frontmatter recommendations.

nodes.title: Relationship
nodes.summary: A first-class edge treated as a node because the relationship itself has history, evidence, dispute, or explanation.

nodes.title: Revision
nodes.summary: An immutable historical version of another node.

nodes.title: Schema
nodes.summary: A rule, ontology, validation document, or governance definition used by XanaNode itself.

nodes.title: Source
nodes.summary: A reference work, transcript, paper, book, talk, article, dataset, or primary evidence item.

nodes.title: Technology
nodes.summary: A tool, protocol, model, platform, architecture, method, or technical system.

nodes.title: Trail
nodes.summary: A curated route through the graph.

nodes.title: Birth date
nodes.summary: Calendar date on which a person, organization, project, or entity began.

nodes.title: Currency value
nodes.summary: A monetary amount with an ISO 4217 currency code.

nodes.title: External identifier
nodes.summary: A typed identifier from an external authority or registry.

nodes.title: Geographic coordinates
nodes.summary: WGS84 latitude and longitude coordinates for a place or geographically located entity.

nodes.title: SI measurement
nodes.summary: A quantified physical or conceptual measurement expressed in SI-compatible units.

nodes.title: Property Registry
nodes.summary: The canonical registry of standardized open-ended node properties for dates, coordinates, money, measurements, and external identifiers.

nodes.title: Property Registry Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for declaring standardized custom node properties such as measurements, coordinates, money values, identifiers, and dates.

nodes.title: Alias of
nodes.summary: The source is an alternative name for the target entity.

nodes.title: Alternative to
nodes.summary: The source is an alternate interpretation, approach, or implementation relative to the target.

nodes.title: Anticipated
nodes.summary: The source anticipated the target without necessarily directly causing it.

nodes.title: Anticipated By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The anticipated_by inverse relationship term derived from anticipated.

nodes.title: Anticipates
nodes.summary: The source foreshadows or historically precedes the target without direct causation.

nodes.title: Appears in
nodes.summary: The source appears in the target work, collection, or event.

nodes.title: Approved
nodes.summary: The source user, reviewer, or process approved the target.

nodes.title: Approved By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The approved_by inverse relationship term derived from approved.

nodes.title: Arrived From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The arrived_from inverse relationship term derived from arrives_at.

nodes.title: Arrives at
nodes.summary: The source reasoning, argument, or inquiry leads to the target conclusion or position.

nodes.title: Authored
nodes.summary: The source authored the target.

nodes.title: Authored By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The authored_by inverse relationship term derived from authored.

nodes.title: Broader than
nodes.summary: The source schema type, concept, or relationship is broader or more general than the target.

nodes.title: Cited By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The cited_by inverse relationship term derived from cites.

nodes.title: Cites
nodes.summary: The source cites the target.

nodes.title: Claim Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The claim_of inverse relationship term derived from has_claim.

nodes.title: Clarified By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The clarified_by inverse relationship term derived from clarifies.

nodes.title: Clarifies
nodes.summary: The source clarifies, resolves ambiguity in, or makes the target more precise.

nodes.title: Coined
nodes.summary: The source coined the target term.

nodes.title: Coined By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The coined_by inverse relationship term derived from coined.

nodes.title: Collaborated with
nodes.summary: The source collaborated with the target.

nodes.title: Compatible with
nodes.summary: The source schema, substrate, type, field, or relationship can interoperate with the target without destructive conversion.

nodes.title: Contains
nodes.summary: The source contains the target.

nodes.title: Contemporary of
nodes.summary: The source existed or worked during the same relevant period as the target.

nodes.title: Context for
nodes.summary: The source provides essential background or framing that makes the target intelligible.

nodes.title: Continued From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The continued_from inverse relationship term derived from continues_to.

nodes.title: Continues to
nodes.summary: The source continues, leads into, or sequences directly to the target.

nodes.title: Contradicted By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The contradicted_by inverse relationship term derived from contradicts.

nodes.title: Contradicts
nodes.summary: The source directly challenges or conflicts with the target.

nodes.title: Contrasts with
nodes.summary: The source is usefully contrasted with the target.

nodes.title: Created
nodes.summary: The source created the target.

nodes.title: Created By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The created_by inverse relationship term derived from created.

nodes.title: Deep Linked From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The deep_linked_from inverse relationship term derived from deep_links_to.

nodes.title: Deep links to
nodes.summary: The source points to a specific addressable fragment of the target.

nodes.title: Defined By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The defined_by inverse relationship term derived from defines.

nodes.title: Defines
nodes.summary: The source gives the defining explanation of the target.

nodes.title: Demonstrated By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The demonstrated_by inverse relationship term derived from demonstrates.

nodes.title: Demonstrates
nodes.summary: The source provides an example or demonstration of the target.

nodes.title: Dependency Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The dependency_of inverse relationship term derived from depends_on.

nodes.title: Depends on
nodes.summary: The source depends on the target.

nodes.title: Depicted By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The depicted_by inverse relationship term derived from depicts.

nodes.title: Depicts
nodes.summary: The source media depicts the target.

nodes.title: Derived from
nodes.summary: The source descends from or is based on the target.

nodes.title: Derived Into Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The derived_into inverse relationship term derived from derived_from.

nodes.title: Discussed In Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The discussed_in inverse relationship term derived from discusses.

nodes.title: Discusses
nodes.summary: The source discusses, explores, or analyzes the target.

nodes.title: Disputed By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The disputed_by inverse relationship term derived from disputes.

nodes.title: Disputed identity
nodes.summary: The source and target have an identity relationship that is contested, ambiguous, or rejected by at least one substrate, contributor, or governance process.

nodes.title: Disputes
nodes.summary: The source disputes the target.

nodes.title: Documented By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The documented_by inverse relationship term derived from documents.

nodes.title: Documents
nodes.summary: The source records evidence about the target.

nodes.title: Enabled By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The enabled_by inverse relationship term derived from enables.

nodes.title: Enables
nodes.summary: The source makes the target possible or easier to achieve.

nodes.title: Equivalent to
nodes.summary: The source is semantically identical or interchangeable with the target.

nodes.title: Evidence for
nodes.summary: The source is evidence for the target claim or relationship.

nodes.title: Explained By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The explained_by inverse relationship term derived from explains.

nodes.title: Explains
nodes.summary: The source provides an explanation of the target â€” its causes, mechanisms, or meaning.

nodes.title: Extended By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The extended_by inverse relationship term derived from extends.

nodes.title: Extends
nodes.summary: The source extends the target.

nodes.title: Extension of
nodes.summary: The source custom schema, type, field, or relationship extends the target core schema, type, field, or relationship.

nodes.title: Featured In Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The featured_in inverse relationship term derived from features.

nodes.title: Featured Speaker Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The featured_speaker inverse relationship term derived from spoke_at.

nodes.title: Features
nodes.summary: The source features the target.

nodes.title: Followed Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The followed inverse relationship term derived from preceded.

nodes.title: Friend of
nodes.summary: The source had a documented friendship with the target.

nodes.title: Had Participant Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The had_participant inverse relationship term derived from participated_in.

nodes.title: Has Alias Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The has_alias inverse relationship term derived from alias_of.

nodes.title: Has Appearance Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The has_appearance inverse relationship term derived from appears_in.

nodes.title: Has claim
nodes.summary: The source concept, essay, observation, or source contains or depends on the target claim.

nodes.title: Has Context Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The has_context inverse relationship term derived from context_for.

nodes.title: Has Evidence Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The has_evidence inverse relationship term derived from evidence_for.

nodes.title: Has Extension Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The has_extension inverse relationship term derived from extension_of.

nodes.title: Has primary media
nodes.summary: The source node uses the target media node as its primary representative image or icon.

nodes.title: Identified
nodes.summary: The source identified, named, or recognized the target.

nodes.title: Identified By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The identified_by inverse relationship term derived from identified.

nodes.title: Implemented By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The implemented_by inverse relationship term derived from implements.

nodes.title: Implements
nodes.summary: The source implements the target idea, technology, protocol, method, or concept.

nodes.title: Included In Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The included_in inverse relationship term derived from includes.

nodes.title: Includes
nodes.summary: The source includes or encompasses the target as a member or component.

nodes.title: Influenced
nodes.summary: The source influenced the target.

nodes.title: Influenced By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The influenced_by inverse relationship term derived from influenced.

nodes.title: Interviewed
nodes.summary: The source interviewed the target.

nodes.title: Interviewed By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The interviewed_by inverse relationship term derived from interviewed.

nodes.title: Introduced
nodes.summary: The source introduced or first formally presented the target.

nodes.title: Introduced By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The introduced_by inverse relationship term derived from introduced.

nodes.title: Introduced in
nodes.summary: The source was introduced, debuted, or first appeared in the target event, work, or venue.

nodes.title: Introduction Venue Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The introduction_venue_of inverse relationship term derived from introduced_in.

nodes.title: Is Start Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The is_start_of inverse relationship term derived from starts_with.

nodes.title: Mapped From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The mapped_from inverse relationship term derived from maps_to.

nodes.title: Maps to
nodes.summary: The source schema type, field, node, or relationship maps to the target schema type, field, node, or relationship for interoperability.

nodes.title: Memorialized By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The memorialized_by inverse relationship term derived from memorializes.

nodes.title: Memorializes
nodes.summary: The source commemorates, honors, or preserves the memory of the target.

nodes.title: Mentioned By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The mentioned_by inverse relationship term derived from mentions.

nodes.title: Mentions
nodes.summary: The source mentions the target without making it structurally central.

nodes.title: Motivated by
nodes.summary: The source was motivated or driven into being by the target.

nodes.title: Motivates Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The motivates inverse relationship term derived from motivated_by.

nodes.title: Narrower Than Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The narrower_than inverse relationship term derived from broader_than.

nodes.title: Occurred at
nodes.summary: The source event occurred at the target place.

nodes.title: Parallel to
nodes.summary: The source developed alongside or resembles the target without necessarily descending from it.

nodes.title: Part Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The part_of inverse relationship term derived from contains.

nodes.title: Participated in
nodes.summary: The source participated in the target.

nodes.title: Popularized
nodes.summary: The source brought the target to a wider audience or made it culturally visible.

nodes.title: Popularized By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The popularized_by inverse relationship term derived from popularized.

nodes.title: Possible match
nodes.summary: The source may represent the same entity, concept, claim, source, or fragment as the target, but the match has not been confirmed.

nodes.title: Preceded
nodes.summary: The source came before the target.

nodes.title: Presented
nodes.summary: The source presented, hosted, narrated, or publicly introduced the target work, program, or media object.

nodes.title: Presented By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The presented_by inverse relationship term derived from presented.

nodes.title: Preserved By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The preserved_by inverse relationship term derived from preserves.

nodes.title: Preserves
nodes.summary: The source preserves the target property, structure, lineage, or context.

nodes.title: Produces Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The produces inverse relationship term derived from results_from.

nodes.title: Proposed
nodes.summary: The source proposed or put forward the target.

nodes.title: Proposed By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The proposed_by inverse relationship term derived from proposed.

nodes.title: Quoted By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The quoted_by inverse relationship term derived from quotes.

nodes.title: Quotes
nodes.summary: The source quotes the target.

nodes.title: Refined By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The refined_by inverse relationship term derived from refines.

nodes.title: Refines
nodes.summary: The source refines, sharpens, or improves the target.

nodes.title: Relationship Type Registry
nodes.summary: The canonical registry of current XanaNode relationship types and inverse semantics.

nodes.title: Related to
nodes.summary: The source is related to the target when no more specific relationship applies.

nodes.title: Represented By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The represented_by inverse relationship term derived from represents.

nodes.title: Represents
nodes.summary: The source media or artifact represents the target.

nodes.title: Required For Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The required_for inverse relationship term derived from requires.

nodes.title: Requires
nodes.summary: The source requires the target as a prerequisite, input, or condition.

nodes.title: Results from
nodes.summary: The source is a consequence, failure mode, output, or downstream effect of the target.

nodes.title: Same entity
nodes.summary: The source and target represent the same real-world entity across substrates, namespaces, versions, or local identifiers.

nodes.title: Shaped By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The shaped_by inverse relationship term derived from shapes.

nodes.title: Shapes
nodes.summary: The source significantly shapes or molds the form, direction, or character of the target.

nodes.title: Site Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The site_of inverse relationship term derived from occurred_at.

nodes.title: Spoke at
nodes.summary: The source person spoke at the target event.

nodes.title: Starts with
nodes.summary: The source sequence, work, or argument begins with the target.

nodes.title: Superseded By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The superseded_by inverse relationship term derived from supersedes.

nodes.title: Supersedes
nodes.summary: The source replaces the target.

nodes.title: Supported By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The supported_by inverse relationship term derived from supports.

nodes.title: Supports
nodes.summary: The source supports the target.

nodes.title: Threatened By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The threatened_by inverse relationship term derived from threatens.

nodes.title: Threatens
nodes.summary: The source threatens, endangers, or poses a risk to the target.

nodes.title: Trained on
nodes.summary: The source system or model was trained on the target dataset, corpus, or collection.

nodes.title: Transcluded By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The transcluded_by inverse relationship term derived from transcludes.

nodes.title: Transcludes
nodes.summary: The source includes a specific versioned fragment of the target by reference rather than copying it, preserving identity, attribution, and revision lineage.

nodes.title: Used As Primary Media For Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_as_primary_media_for inverse relationship term derived from has_primary_media.

nodes.title: Used By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_by inverse relationship term derived from uses.

nodes.title: Used To Train Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_to_train inverse relationship term derived from trained_on.

nodes.title: Uses
nodes.summary: The source uses, employs, or relies on the target.

nodes.title: RO-Crate Metadata Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for Research Object Crate metadata used to describe packaged substrate artifacts and their provenance.

nodes.title: Substrate Diff Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for representing portable differences between substrate states.

nodes.title: Substrate Manifest Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining substrate manifests, repository metadata, federation hints, imports, trust data, and pack metadata.

nodes.title: Substrate Node Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining portable node records, including type, subtype, facets, media metadata, source snapshots, fragments, and relationships.

nodes.title: Substrate Relationships Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining portable relationship records, including asserted time, validity time, confidence, evidence, and tumbler references.

nodes.title: GitHub Profile
nodes.summary: The public GitHub profile collecting XanaNode development activity and repositories.

nodes.title: Hugo Official Site
nodes.summary: The official website and documentation source for Hugo.

nodes.title: Support XanaNode
nodes.summary: A support link for people who want to help sustain authored knowledge substrate creation.

nodes.title: XanaNode.com
nodes.summary: The primary public home for the personally authored canonical XanaNode substrate.

nodes.title: XanaNode Core SDK Repository
nodes.summary: The public Core SDK repository for validating, building, loading, analyzing, and exporting XanaNode protocol artifacts across renderers and tools.

nodes.title: XanaNode Hugo Repository
nodes.summary: The public Hugo projection repository that renders XanaNode substrates as static readable pages and an interactive graph.

nodes.title: XanaNode.net
nodes.summary: A public XanaNode domain reserved for network, federation, or infrastructure presentation.

nodes.title: XanaNode.org
nodes.summary: A public XanaNode domain reserved for protocol, community, governance, or organizational presentation.

nodes.title: XanaNode Protocol Repository
nodes.summary: The public repository for the XanaNode protocol schemas, specifications, governance documents, registries, proposals, and examples.

nodes.title: XanaNode Studio Repository
nodes.summary: The public Studio repository reserved for richer local-first authoring and review interfaces over XanaNode substrates.

nodes.title: XanaNode Workspace Repository
nodes.summary: The public workspace repository for coordinating local substrates, mounted packs, author workflows, media handling, and stack orchestration.

nodes.title: Cytoscape.js
nodes.summary: A graph visualization library used by the XanaNode Hugo projection to render interactive substrate graphs.

nodes.title: Git
nodes.summary: The distributed version control system used to preserve substrate history, protocol revisions, repositories, and deployment workflows.

nodes.title: Hugo
nodes.summary: A static site generator used by the XanaNode Hugo projection to publish substrates as static websites.

nodes.title: Node.js
nodes.summary: The JavaScript runtime used by XanaNode Core, Hugo preparation scripts, and build tooling.

nodes.title: Python
nodes.summary: A language used by the protocol repository validation tooling and supported as a future validation/runtime target.

nodes.title: XanaNode Stack Trail
nodes.summary: A curated route from the XanaNode concept through protocol, Core, projection layers, Hugo, workspace, sources, and support.
